title: ctenopteris capillaris
intro: Ctenopteris capillaris, also known as the maidenhair fern, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. It is native to Australia and New Zealand, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be beneficial for treating wounds and skin conditions. Ctenopteris capillaris can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also used in topical creams and ointments to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
